title: trifolium albopurpureum var. columbinum
intro: Trifolium albopurpureum var. columbinum, commonly known as the white wild indigo or prairie indigo, is a perennial legume native to North America. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health concerns. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Trifolium albopurpureum var. columbinum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
